extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won a crosswind-shattered sprint after ALAPHILIPPE's front riding at 38km fractured the peloton, eliminating PINOT and PORTE from GC contention.
tactical_patterns: ROWE Luke with BURGHARDT Marcus initiated crosswind split at 68km, catching GC riders WELLENS, PINOT off guard despite moderate breakaway gap.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian riding at front himself at 38km intensified echelon splitting, unusual yellow jersey aggression on presumed sprint stage.
EF Education First with CLARKE Simon orchestrated tempo after initial crosswind, showing coordinated echelon exploitation beyond single-team Ineos domination pattern.

rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: Lost 1:40 in crosswinds despite starting stage third overall, catastrophic positioning failure from presumed GC podium contender.
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: Caught behind crosswind split, unable to close 12-second gap despite LUTSENKO help, exposing isolation from Trek support.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Lost over 2 minutes behind even Pinot group, complete tactical and positional disaster worse than other GC casualties.
rider_signals.BENNETT George: negative: Isolated at 1:51 after bottle duty, Jumbo-Visma prioritized Van Aert sprint over protecting GC rider positioning.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Aggressively rode front at 38km in yellow jersey on flat terrain, atypical puncher behavior that gained GC time.
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Beat pure sprinters VIVIANI and EWEN after surviving crosswind selection, showing classics power translates to Tour sprints post-echelon.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Made front split gaining time on rivals, showing Ineos positioning remained superior despite ROWE initiating move.
